Okay, so I need to figure out if it's legally valid for a man to leave all his money to his widow's sister, who he plans to marry next year. Let me break this down step by step.

First, I know that in many places, you can't legally inherit from someone you're going to marry because you're not married yet. Inheritance usually requires a legal relationship at the time of death. So if the man dies before the marriage, and his future wife (now his widow's sister) is named in the will, is that possible?

But the key point here is that if he dies before the marriage, can he leave his money to someone who is going to be his wife, but isn't yet? So in that case, she would receive the inheritance as named in the will. But if he dies after they get married, then the laws of intestacy (if he didn't have a will) would give her a portion of his estate, unless he left her out in the will, which is possible.

But the scenario is that he leaves all his money to his widow's sister (his future wife) in the will, and he plans to marry her next year. In some jurisdictions, a spouse is considered a forced heir, meaning they are entitled to a certain portion of the estate regardless of the will. So even if the man left everything to his new wife's sister, the new wife may still be entitled to a portion of the estate by law. This is common in civil law countries, but less so in common law countries like the United States and the United Kingdom.
